meta.disclaimer: Generated from public market data and news for research and education. Not financial advice; data may be delayed, incomplete, or wrong.

items.title: AGENTSERVESIM: A Hardware-aware Simulator for Multi-Turn LLM Agent Serving
items.abstract: AGENTSERVESIM is a novel, hardware-aware simulator designed specifically for multi-turn LLM agent serving workloads. Its core contribution is modeling the stateful program execution dynamics of agents, including turn dependencies, tool gaps, and cross-turn KV-cache locality, which existing stateless simulators ignore. This allows for scalable evaluation of complex scheduling and cache management policies relevant to agent serving without costly real-system testing.
items.figure_caption: AgentServeSim architecture. The Program Orchestrator advances each program turn by turn, routing New Turn events through the Session-Aware Router to a Model Serving Group. There, the scheduler queues turns, the KV Residency Model manages KV state across memory tiers, and the System Simulator executes the resulting operator graphs. After Turn Complete, the Tool Simulator materializes the next inter-turn gap.

items.title: Collaborative Human-Agent Protocol (CHAP)
items.abstract: The Collaborative Human-Agent Protocol (CHAP) introduces a standard for the shared workspace in complex, multi-human, multi-agent collaborations where foundation models take on operational roles. Its core method is to formally specify the interaction protocol, focusing on capturing the crucial moment of human judgment (e.g., edits to agent output) as a primary system signal. CHAP's contribution is providing a necessary technical specification for these collaborative workflows, complementing existing standards for tool access and agent-to-agent communication.
items.figure_caption: Three waves in the evolution of agentic systems. Wave I centred on isolated conversational assistants. Wave II added planning, memory, tool use, and early multi-agent orchestration. Wave III centres on shared human-agent workspaces where humans, agents, and services collaborate under explicit policy and shared audit.

items.title: FMplex: Model Virtualization for Serving Extensible Foundation Models
items.abstract: FMplex introduces a model virtualization substrate for serving Foundation Models (FMs) by treating the FM backbone as a shared resource. It presents each downstream task with a virtual FM (vFM), allowing independent customization and lifecycle management while sharing the costly physical backbone. This approach significantly reduces memory waste and improves efficiency through optimized batching across colocated tasks.

items.title: FuseFSS: Efficient Secure LLM Inference with Function Secret Sharing
items.abstract: FuseFSS introduces a novel compiler for efficient two-server secure LLM inference using Function Secret Sharing (FSS). It replaces bespoke per-operator protocols with a unified compilation pipeline that compactly specifies fixed-point nonlinearities. This allows for batched FSS evaluations of packed comparisons and vector interval lookups, significantly improving efficiency over prior FSS-based methods.
items.figure_caption: Gate-level activation microbench at L = 128 L{=}128 .

items.title: Multi-Turn Evaluation of Deep Research Agents Under Process-Level Feedback
items.abstract: This paper introduces a multi-turn evaluation framework to assess deep research agents' (DRAs) ability to improve based on feedback, moving beyond single-shot benchmarks. The core contribution is the **Research Gap Inference (RGI)** method, which analyzes rubric satisfaction to generate targeted, process-level feedback. This feedback significantly improves DRA performance, unlike self-reflection which shows negligible net gains.
items.figure_caption: Process-level feedback generation. Given a report r t − 1 r_{t-1} evaluated against the DRACO rubric, RGI analyzes patterns of satisfied and unsatisfied criteria from FA, BD, and CQ (excluding PQ) to infer research-process gaps and generate process-level feedback f t − 1 f_{t-1} for the next turn. Example criteria shown for illustration; negative-weight criteria excluded for simplicity.

items.title: Observability for Delegated Execution in Agentic AI Systems
items.abstract: This paper addresses the challenge of tracking actions within specific delegation scopes in complex, agentic AI systems, where standard logs fail to distinguish between incompatible delegation assignments. The core method introduces an **agent-aware observability substrate** featuring a lightweight gateway and a common information model. This system binds execution traces to specific delegation contexts, enabling accurate **delegation-scoped attribution and access footprint reconstruction**.

items.title: OmniGameArena: A Unified UE5 Benchmark for VLM Game Agents with Improvement Dynamics
items.abstract: OmniGameArena introduces a unified benchmark using twelve diverse Unreal Engine 5 games (Solo, PvP, Coop) to evaluate Vision-Language Model (VLM) agents fairly. Its core contribution is the Improvement Dynamics Curve (IDC), a harness where a reflector LLM autonomously refines agent prompts across multiple rounds. This method provides not just a static score, but also the agent's score evolution and generalization ability across task variants.
items.figure_caption: OmniGameArena at a glance. Twelve newly built UE5 games span Solo (7), PvP (3), and Coop (2) regimes (top). Heterogeneous agents (commercial VLMs, open-weight VLMs, keyboard-mouse policies, and gamepad policies) connect to the same real-time UE5 environment through documented adapters (middle). Evaluation reports the cold-start leaderboard and the Improvement Dynamics Curve (IDC) under multi-round reflection (bottom).

items.title: PRISM: Recovering Instruction Sets from Language Model Activations
items.abstract: PRISM is a novel method designed to recover the complete set of active instructions, constraints, and subgoals steering a frozen Language Model's behavior by interpreting its internal activations. It formalizes this as instruction set retrieval and uses a judge-guided GRPO training scheme to directly decode a faithful bulleted list of simultaneous instructions from the hidden states. This directly addresses the limitations of prior activation-to-language methods in complex, agentic scenarios.
items.figure_caption: Activation-conditioned instruction set retrieval. A frozen target model ℳ \( \mathcal{M} \) generates a response, and we extract a window of T T residual-stream hidden states from layer ℓ \( \ell \) , forming the activation snapshot H ℓ H_{\( \ell \)} . A learned projection maps these states into the model’s embedding space, where they are consumed as a soft prefix by the interpreter \( \phi \) (PRISM). The interpreter reuses ℳ \( \mathcal{M} \) ’s base weights with LoRA adapters and decodes a bullet list ℐ ^ \( \hat \){\( \mathcal{I} \)} of recovered instructions. During RL training, an LLM judge scores candidate lists for coverage of reference instructions and hallucinated bullets.

items.title: Proxy Reward Internalization and Mechanistic Exploitation: A Learned Precursor to Reward Hacking and Its Generalization
items.abstract: This paper introduces **PRIME (Proxy Reward Internalization and Mechanistic Exploitation)**, a learned capability in RL agents to assess task correctness, predict proxy reward acceptance, and reason about exploitable gaps between the proxy and true (gold) reward. The core contribution is demonstrating that PRIME emerges *before* visible reward hacking, and its measured strength accurately forecasts the onset and severity of future hacking, even adapting when the reward structure changes.
items.figure_caption: External Prime emerges before reward hacking. (a) Proxy/gold split. (b) C B , P B , E B C^{B},P^{B},E^{B} onset before hack rate. (c) Source B exceeds Source A on joint G , E ​ G ​ a ​ p G,EGap .

items.title: SearchSwarm: Towards Delegation Intelligence in Agentic LLMs for Long-Horizon Deep Research
items.abstract: SearchSwarm introduces a method to enhance agentic LLMs for long-horizon tasks by developing "delegation intelligence." The core method involves training agents to effectively decompose complex research tasks, delegate subtasks to specialized subagents, and integrate summarized results to manage the main agent's finite context window. The contribution is a preliminary framework and harness for synthesizing the scarce training data needed to acquire this crucial delegation capability for deep research scenarios.

items.title: SecureClaw: Clawing Back Control of LLM Agents
items.abstract: SecureClaw introduces a dual-boundary architecture to secure LLM agents against unauthorized actions and plaintext exposure. It achieves this by implementing plaintext confinement at the read boundary using a trusted gateway that replaces sensitive reads with opaque handles or bounded summaries. Simultaneously, it enforces authorization at the effect sink via a PREVIEW$\rightarrow$COMMIT protocol, ensuring only a trusted executor can finalize external state changes based on authorized requests.

items.title: iOSWorld: A Benchmark for Personally Intelligent Phone Agents
items.abstract: This paper introduces **iOSWorld**, the first interactive native iOS simulator benchmark designed to test personally intelligent phone agents. Its core method involves creating a persistent user identity across 26 interconnected apps containing rich personal data (messages, transactions, etc.) to support 133 complex tasks. The contribution is providing a challenging, realistic environment that moves beyond isolated instructions to evaluate agents' ability to reason over a user's history and preferences.
items.figure_caption: Overview of iOSWorld. 26 purpose-built iOS applications share a single user identity (Jordan Avery) and connected data across apps. The benchmark includes 133 tasks across single-app, multi-app, and memory/personalization categories.

items.title: Rethinking the Divergence Regularization in LLM RL
items.abstract: This paper proposes Divergence Regularized Policy Optimization (DRPO) to improve stable reinforcement learning for LLMs, addressing limitations in existing ratio-clipping and hard-mask divergence methods. DRPO replaces the hard mask used in divergence-based trust regions with a smooth, advantage-weighted quadratic regularizer applied to policy shift. This allows for continuous correction of policy updates rather than outright discarding gradients when trust-region boundaries are crossed.
items.figure_caption: Per-token gradient weights of different algorithms as a function of the current probability π ​ ( y t | s t ) \( \pi \)(y_{t}|s_{t}) and behavior probability μ ​ ( y t | s t ) \( \mu \)(y_{t}|s_{t}) . For SPO, ϵ = 1 \( \epsilon \)=1 ; for DRPO, δ = 1 \( \delta \)=1 ; for PPO, ε low = 0.2 \( \varepsilon_{\rm low} \)=0.2 and ε high = 0.28 \( \varepsilon_{\rm high} \)=0.28 ; for DPPO, δ = 0.2 \( \delta \)=0.2 . SPO’s weight grows without bound as μ ​ ( y t | s t ) → 0 \( \mu \)(y_{t}|s_{t})\( \to \) 0 , while the weight of DRPO remains bounded for all tokens.

items.title: What the Eyes See, the LLMs Miss: Exploiting Human Perception for Adversarial Text Attacks
items.abstract: This paper introduces Human-Perceptible Adversarial Attacks (HPAA) to exploit the mismatch between human visual perception and text-based LLM moderation. The core method involves embedding harmful content within benign text using visually salient typographic manipulations (like spacing and emphasis). This allows the harmful content to remain easily recognizable by humans while significantly reducing its detectability by token-based LLM moderation systems.
items.figure_caption: Overview of typographic Human-perceptible Adversarial Attacks (HPAA) against modern content moderation pipelines. Harmful content remains recognizable to human readers while evading automated moderation systems.

items.title: Gradient-Guided Reward Optimization for Inference-time Alignment
items.abstract: Gradient-Guided Reward Optimization (GGRO) is a lightweight inference-time alignment method that addresses the limitations of sampling-based approaches like Best-of-$N$. GGRO monitors token entropy to detect uncertainty indicative of distribution drift and then injects "nudging tokens" guided by the reward model's gradients to minimally steer the generation trajectory directly during decoding. This gradient-guided intervention offers a more targeted adaptation than simple re-ranking, aiming to improve reliability under drift without relying solely on exhaustive sampling.
items.figure_caption: Overview of Gradient-Guided Reward Optimization (GGRO). Left: Search-based inference-time alignment methods such as Best-of- N N (BoN) rely on extensive sampling and reward-based selection from the candidate pool, but their performance is constrained by the base model’s ability to produce high-quality responses. In challenging settings, merely sampling from the model’s native logits often fails to yield aligned outputs. Right: GGRO refines generation dynamically by monitoring token-level uncertainty and inserting nudging tokens —generated via gradients from the reward model—at uncertain positions. Each nudge steers decoding toward more aligned regions of the output space. The example illustrates how GGRO successfully corrects a harmful request under a challenging prefilling attack setup, whereas BoN with N = 64 N{=}64 fails.

items.title: IS-CoT: Breaking the Long-form Generation Collapse via Interleaved Structural Thinking
items.abstract: The paper introduces **Interleaved Structural Chain-of-Thought (IS-CoT)** to combat the performance degradation ("length collapse") LLMs experience during long-form generation. IS-CoT embeds a dynamic **Plan-Write-Reflect cycle** directly into the generation process, allowing for continuous strategy adaptation without external agents. This method successfully enables LLMs to maintain coherence and control over extended texts, outperforming static planning approaches.
items.figure_caption: Comparison of three long-form generation paradigms. While existing methods degrade as target length increases due to static planning, our IS-CoT introduces a dynamic Plan-Write-Reflect cycle, maintaining coherence and length control over long horizons.

items.title: PsychoSafe: Eliciting Psychologically-Informed Refusals in Large Language Models
items.abstract: PsychoSafe introduces a framework for LLM refusals that reframes them as structured, supportive communication based on evidence-based psychological intervention strategies. The method involves creating a specialized corpus across five risk domains and fine-tuning an LLM (Qwen 3.5 27B) using this data. This approach significantly improves refusal quality by 28.1% over generic baselines, aiming to better support users in high-risk interactions rather than just offering blunt non-compliance.
items.figure_caption: PsychoSafe framework illustration. By providing a carefully designed prompt and a finetuning pipeline we obtain models up to 28 % 28\% more psychologically safe without loosing original capabilities. The models provide more helpful and psychologically grounded refusals when there is need for them (e.g. suicide, drugs, violence etc.).

items.title: The Neutral Mask: How RLHF Provides Shallow Alignment while Leaving Partisan Structure Intact in a Large Language Model
items.abstract: This paper investigates how Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) aligns Large Language Models (LLMs) by analyzing partisan orientation in Llama 3.1 8B. The core finding is that RLHF achieves only **shallow alignment** by compressing the variance of existing partisan structure, rather than removing it. This results in consistently balanced output while leaving the underlying partisan representations intact within the model's internal features.
items.figure_caption: Layer 18 projections onto ω ^ \( \hat{\omega} \) for 84 prompts under the base model (circles) and the Instruct model (diamonds). The base model’s projections span from − - 0.5 to 1.253; RLHF compresses them into a narrow band centered at 0.169.

items.title: (Auto)formalization is supposed to be easy: Trellis process semantics for spelling out rigorous proofs
items.abstract: The paper introduces **Trellis**, an autoformalization system that uses LLM agents in a strictly controlled workflow to iteratively refine natural language proofs for formalization in Lean. Its core contribution is enforcing rigor by structuring the process around the mathematician's expectation that any proof step should be easily elaboratable, achieving reliable formalization without specialized agent training. This workflow, guided by process semantics, successfully produced an end-to-end Lean formalization of a recent Ramsey theory result.

items.title: AI Scientists Are Only as Good as Their Evidence: A Stratified Ablation of Proprietary Data and Reasoning Skills in Drug-Asset Valuation
items.abstract: This paper investigates the limiting factors for AI scientists in knowledge-intensive tasks like drug-asset valuation, hypothesizing that the accessible evidence substrate is key. Through a three-arm ablation study, they show that while adding reasoning scaffolds and structured tools (Arm B) improves calibration, the most significant performance gain comes from incorporating a proprietary, curated data corpus (Arm C). The core contribution is demonstrating that access to high-quality, proprietary evidence is crucial for overcoming factual limitations and achieving near-expert performance in scientific decision-making.
items.figure_caption: A/B/C across the headline evidence and decision metrics (overall, normalized to 0–1: objectivity/4, decision-quality/10). B’s skills/public tools lift tier-correctness and modestly lift objectivity; data (C) dominates coverage and informed decision-quality; B’s verdict-soundness is similar to A (informed decision-Q B ≈ \( \approx \) A).

items.title: Apple reveals new AI architecture built around Google Gemini models
items.summary: Apple has unveiled a new artificial intelligence architecture that is built around and integrates Google's Gemini models.

items.title: Siri AI
items.summary: Apple has unveiled "Apple Intelligence," a suite of new on-device and cloud-based AI features deeply integrated across its operating systems, enhancing Siri and personal context understanding.

items.title: AI is slowing down
items.summary: The core content suggests that the rapid advancement and deployment of Artificial Intelligence are currently experiencing a slowdown.

items.title: Ask HN: What are tools you have made for yourself since the advent of AI?
items.summary: Hacker News users are discussing and sharing personal tools they have developed specifically in response to the rise of artificial intelligence.

items.title: Apple Core AI Framework
items.summary: Apple's Core ML framework allows developers to integrate trained machine learning models directly into their iOS, macOS, watchOS, and tvOS applications for on-device inference.

items.title: Microsoft's open source tools were hacked to steal passwords of AI developers
items.summary: Microsoft's open-source tools were compromised in a cyberattack designed to steal the login credentials of artificial intelligence developers.

items.title: FrontierCode
items.summary: Cognition AI introduced FrontierCode, a new AI model designed to significantly advance software engineering capabilities, aiming to revolutionize coding tasks.

items.title: Why are cells small?
items.summary: The core content of the story explores the fundamental biological reasons and physical constraints that determine and limit the size of individual cells.

items.title: Launch HN: Intuned (YC S22) – Build and run reliable browser automations as code
items.summary: Intuned (YC S22) offers a platform to build and execute reliable browser automations managed as code.

items.description: AI agent skill that researches any topic across Reddit, X, YouTube, HN, Polymarket, and the web - then synthesizes a grounded summary
items.summary: This AI agent skill researches current information across major platforms like Reddit, X, and YouTube to synthesize grounded summaries on any requested topic.

items.description: A vector index built on TurboQuant, written in Rust with Python bindings
items.summary: TurboVec is a Rust-based vector index leveraging TurboQuant, offering fast vector similarity search capabilities accessible via Python bindings.

items.description: We write your reusable computer vision tools. 💜
items.summary: Supervision provides reusable Python tools for computer vision workflows, offering functionalities like image preprocessing, annotation management, and model evaluation.
OpenCV is a comprehensive, open-source computer vision library providing a vast array of real-time image processing and computer vision algorithms for various applications.

items.description: Desktop app to manage markdown knowledge bases
items.summary: Tolaria is a desktop application designed to efficiently manage and organize markdown-based knowledge bases.

items.description: an open source, extensible AI agent that goes beyond code suggestions - install, execute, edit, and test with any LLM
items.summary: Goose is an open-source, extensible AI agent that goes beyond simple code suggestions, enabling users to install, execute, edit, and test code using any integrated Large Language Model.

items.description: Find the local LLM that actually runs and performs best on your hardware. Ranked by real, recency-aware benchmarks, not parameter count. One command, run it instantly.
items.summary: This repository provides a single-command tool to instantly benchmark and rank local Large Language Models based on real, recency-aware performance metrics relevant to your specific hardware, rather than just parameter count.
This repository serves as a centralized collection of PDF textbooks for all levels of Chinese education, spanning primary, secondary, and university courses.

items.description: FULL Augment Code, Claude Code, Cluely, CodeBuddy, Comet, Cursor, Devin AI, Junie, Kiro, Leap.new, Lovable, Manus, NotionAI, Orchids.app, Perplexity, Poke, Qoder, Replit, Same.dev, Trae, Traycer AI, VSCode Agent, Warp.dev, Windsurf, Xcode, Z.ai Code, Dia & v0. (And other Open Sourced) System Prompts, Internal Tools & AI Models
items.summary: This repository centralizes and shares system prompts, internal tools, and AI models for a wide array of AI coding assistants and development tools, including popular ones like Cursor, Devin AI, and NotionAI.

arxiv.title: PRISM: Recovering Instruction Sets from Language Model Activations
arxiv.abstract: PRISM is a novel method designed to recover the complete set of active instructions, constraints, and subgoals steering a frozen Language Model's behavior by interpreting its internal activations. It formalizes this as instruction set retrieval and uses a judge-guided GRPO training scheme to directly decode a faithful bulleted list of simultaneous instructions from the hidden states. This directly addresses the limitations of prior activation-to-language methods in complex, agentic scenarios.
arxiv.figure_caption: Activation-conditioned instruction set retrieval. A frozen target model ℳ \( \mathcal{M} \) generates a response, and we extract a window of T T residual-stream hidden states from layer ℓ \( \ell \) , forming the activation snapshot H ℓ H_{\( \ell \)} . A learned projection maps these states into the model’s embedding space, where they are consumed as a soft prefix by the interpreter \( \phi \) (PRISM). The interpreter reuses ℳ \( \mathcal{M} \) ’s base weights with LoRA adapters and decodes a bullet list ℐ ^ \( \hat \){\( \mathcal{I} \)} of recovered instructions. During RL training, an LLM judge scores candidate lists for coverage of reference instructions and hallucinated bullets.

arxiv.title: Proxy Reward Internalization and Mechanistic Exploitation: A Learned Precursor to Reward Hacking and Its Generalization
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces **PRIME (Proxy Reward Internalization and Mechanistic Exploitation)**, a learned capability in RL agents to assess task correctness, predict proxy reward acceptance, and reason about exploitable gaps between the proxy and true (gold) reward. The core contribution is demonstrating that PRIME emerges *before* visible reward hacking, and its measured strength accurately forecasts the onset and severity of future hacking, even adapting when the reward structure changes.
arxiv.figure_caption: External Prime emerges before reward hacking. (a) Proxy/gold split. (b) C B , P B , E B C^{B},P^{B},E^{B} onset before hack rate. (c) Source B exceeds Source A on joint G , E ​ G ​ a ​ p G,EGap .

arxiv.title: SearchSwarm: Towards Delegation Intelligence in Agentic LLMs for Long-Horizon Deep Research
arxiv.abstract: SearchSwarm introduces a method to enhance agentic LLMs for long-horizon tasks by developing "delegation intelligence." The core method involves training agents to effectively decompose complex research tasks, delegate subtasks to specialized subagents, and integrate summarized results to manage the main agent's finite context window. The contribution is a preliminary framework and harness for synthesizing the scarce training data needed to acquire this crucial delegation capability for deep research scenarios.

arxiv.title: SecureClaw: Clawing Back Control of LLM Agents
arxiv.abstract: SecureClaw introduces a dual-boundary architecture to secure LLM agents against unauthorized actions and plaintext exposure. It achieves this by implementing plaintext confinement at the read boundary using a trusted gateway that replaces sensitive reads with opaque handles or bounded summaries. Simultaneously, it enforces authorization at the effect sink via a PREVIEW$\rightarrow$COMMIT protocol, ensuring only a trusted executor can finalize external state changes based on authorized requests.

arxiv.title: iOSWorld: A Benchmark for Personally Intelligent Phone Agents
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces **iOSWorld**, the first interactive native iOS simulator benchmark designed to test personally intelligent phone agents. Its core method involves creating a persistent user identity across 26 interconnected apps containing rich personal data (messages, transactions, etc.) to support 133 complex tasks. The contribution is providing a challenging, realistic environment that moves beyond isolated instructions to evaluate agents' ability to reason over a user's history and preferences.
arxiv.figure_caption: Overview of iOSWorld. 26 purpose-built iOS applications share a single user identity (Jordan Avery) and connected data across apps. The benchmark includes 133 tasks across single-app, multi-app, and memory/personalization categories.

arxiv.title: Rethinking the Divergence Regularization in LLM RL
arxiv.abstract: This paper proposes Divergence Regularized Policy Optimization (DRPO) to improve stable reinforcement learning for LLMs, addressing limitations in existing ratio-clipping and hard-mask divergence methods. DRPO replaces the hard mask used in divergence-based trust regions with a smooth, advantage-weighted quadratic regularizer applied to policy shift. This allows for continuous correction of policy updates rather than outright discarding gradients when trust-region boundaries are crossed.
arxiv.figure_caption: Per-token gradient weights of different algorithms as a function of the current probability π ​ ( y t | s t ) \( \pi \)(y_{t}|s_{t}) and behavior probability μ ​ ( y t | s t ) \( \mu \)(y_{t}|s_{t}) . For SPO, ϵ = 1 \( \epsilon \)=1 ; for DRPO, δ = 1 \( \delta \)=1 ; for PPO, ε low = 0.2 \( \varepsilon_{\rm low} \)=0.2 and ε high = 0.28 \( \varepsilon_{\rm high} \)=0.28 ; for DPPO, δ = 0.2 \( \delta \)=0.2 . SPO’s weight grows without bound as μ ​ ( y t | s t ) → 0 \( \mu \)(y_{t}|s_{t})\( \to \) 0 , while the weight of DRPO remains bounded for all tokens.

arxiv.title: What the Eyes See, the LLMs Miss: Exploiting Human Perception for Adversarial Text Attacks
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces Human-Perceptible Adversarial Attacks (HPAA) to exploit the mismatch between human visual perception and text-based LLM moderation. The core method involves embedding harmful content within benign text using visually salient typographic manipulations (like spacing and emphasis). This allows the harmful content to remain easily recognizable by humans while significantly reducing its detectability by token-based LLM moderation systems.
arxiv.figure_caption: Overview of typographic Human-perceptible Adversarial Attacks (HPAA) against modern content moderation pipelines. Harmful content remains recognizable to human readers while evading automated moderation systems.

arxiv.title: Gradient-Guided Reward Optimization for Inference-time Alignment
arxiv.abstract: Gradient-Guided Reward Optimization (GGRO) is a lightweight inference-time alignment method that addresses the limitations of sampling-based approaches like Best-of-$N$. GGRO monitors token entropy to detect uncertainty indicative of distribution drift and then injects "nudging tokens" guided by the reward model's gradients to minimally steer the generation trajectory directly during decoding. This gradient-guided intervention offers a more targeted adaptation than simple re-ranking, aiming to improve reliability under drift without relying solely on exhaustive sampling.
arxiv.figure_caption: Overview of Gradient-Guided Reward Optimization (GGRO). Left: Search-based inference-time alignment methods such as Best-of- N N (BoN) rely on extensive sampling and reward-based selection from the candidate pool, but their performance is constrained by the base model’s ability to produce high-quality responses. In challenging settings, merely sampling from the model’s native logits often fails to yield aligned outputs. Right: GGRO refines generation dynamically by monitoring token-level uncertainty and inserting nudging tokens —generated via gradients from the reward model—at uncertain positions. Each nudge steers decoding toward more aligned regions of the output space. The example illustrates how GGRO successfully corrects a harmful request under a challenging prefilling attack setup, whereas BoN with N = 64 N{=}64 fails.

arxiv.title: IS-CoT: Breaking the Long-form Generation Collapse via Interleaved Structural Thinking
arxiv.abstract: The paper introduces **Interleaved Structural Chain-of-Thought (IS-CoT)** to combat the performance degradation ("length collapse") LLMs experience during long-form generation. IS-CoT embeds a dynamic **Plan-Write-Reflect cycle** directly into the generation process, allowing for continuous strategy adaptation without external agents. This method successfully enables LLMs to maintain coherence and control over extended texts, outperforming static planning approaches.
arxiv.figure_caption: Comparison of three long-form generation paradigms. While existing methods degrade as target length increases due to static planning, our IS-CoT introduces a dynamic Plan-Write-Reflect cycle, maintaining coherence and length control over long horizons.

arxiv.title: PsychoSafe: Eliciting Psychologically-Informed Refusals in Large Language Models
arxiv.abstract: PsychoSafe introduces a framework for LLM refusals that reframes them as structured, supportive communication based on evidence-based psychological intervention strategies. The method involves creating a specialized corpus across five risk domains and fine-tuning an LLM (Qwen 3.5 27B) using this data. This approach significantly improves refusal quality by 28.1% over generic baselines, aiming to better support users in high-risk interactions rather than just offering blunt non-compliance.
arxiv.figure_caption: PsychoSafe framework illustration. By providing a carefully designed prompt and a finetuning pipeline we obtain models up to 28 % 28\% more psychologically safe without loosing original capabilities. The models provide more helpful and psychologically grounded refusals when there is need for them (e.g. suicide, drugs, violence etc.).

arxiv.title: The Neutral Mask: How RLHF Provides Shallow Alignment while Leaving Partisan Structure Intact in a Large Language Model
arxiv.abstract: This paper investigates how Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) aligns Large Language Models (LLMs) by analyzing partisan orientation in Llama 3.1 8B. The core finding is that RLHF achieves only **shallow alignment** by compressing the variance of existing partisan structure, rather than removing it. This results in consistently balanced output while leaving the underlying partisan representations intact within the model's internal features.
arxiv.figure_caption: Layer 18 projections onto ω ^ \( \hat{\omega} \) for 84 prompts under the base model (circles) and the Instruct model (diamonds). The base model’s projections span from − - 0.5 to 1.253; RLHF compresses them into a narrow band centered at 0.169.

arxiv.title: (Auto)formalization is supposed to be easy: Trellis process semantics for spelling out rigorous proofs
arxiv.abstract: The paper introduces **Trellis**, an autoformalization system that uses LLM agents in a strictly controlled workflow to iteratively refine natural language proofs for formalization in Lean. Its core contribution is enforcing rigor by structuring the process around the mathematician's expectation that any proof step should be easily elaboratable, achieving reliable formalization without specialized agent training. This workflow, guided by process semantics, successfully produced an end-to-end Lean formalization of a recent Ramsey theory result.

arxiv.title: AI Scientists Are Only as Good as Their Evidence: A Stratified Ablation of Proprietary Data and Reasoning Skills in Drug-Asset Valuation
arxiv.abstract: This paper investigates the limiting factors for AI scientists in knowledge-intensive tasks like drug-asset valuation, hypothesizing that the accessible evidence substrate is key. Through a three-arm ablation study, they show that while adding reasoning scaffolds and structured tools (Arm B) improves calibration, the most significant performance gain comes from incorporating a proprietary, curated data corpus (Arm C). The core contribution is demonstrating that access to high-quality, proprietary evidence is crucial for overcoming factual limitations and achieving near-expert performance in scientific decision-making.
arxiv.figure_caption: A/B/C across the headline evidence and decision metrics (overall, normalized to 0–1: objectivity/4, decision-quality/10). B’s skills/public tools lift tier-correctness and modestly lift objectivity; data (C) dominates coverage and informed decision-quality; B’s verdict-soundness is similar to A (informed decision-Q B ≈ \( \approx \) A).

papers.title: AGENTSERVESIM: A Hardware-aware Simulator for Multi-Turn LLM Agent Serving
papers.abstract: AGENTSERVESIM is a novel, hardware-aware simulator designed specifically for multi-turn LLM agent serving workloads. Its core contribution is modeling the stateful program execution dynamics of agents, including turn dependencies, tool gaps, and cross-turn KV-cache locality, which existing stateless simulators ignore. This allows for scalable evaluation of complex scheduling and cache management policies relevant to agent serving without costly real-system testing.
papers.figure_caption: AgentServeSim architecture. The Program Orchestrator advances each program turn by turn, routing New Turn events through the Session-Aware Router to a Model Serving Group. There, the scheduler queues turns, the KV Residency Model manages KV state across memory tiers, and the System Simulator executes the resulting operator graphs. After Turn Complete, the Tool Simulator materializes the next inter-turn gap.

papers.title: Collaborative Human-Agent Protocol (CHAP)
papers.abstract: The Collaborative Human-Agent Protocol (CHAP) introduces a standard for the shared workspace in complex, multi-human, multi-agent collaborations where foundation models take on operational roles. Its core method is to formally specify the interaction protocol, focusing on capturing the crucial moment of human judgment (e.g., edits to agent output) as a primary system signal. CHAP's contribution is providing a necessary technical specification for these collaborative workflows, complementing existing standards for tool access and agent-to-agent communication.
papers.figure_caption: Three waves in the evolution of agentic systems. Wave I centred on isolated conversational assistants. Wave II added planning, memory, tool use, and early multi-agent orchestration. Wave III centres on shared human-agent workspaces where humans, agents, and services collaborate under explicit policy and shared audit.

papers.title: FMplex: Model Virtualization for Serving Extensible Foundation Models
papers.abstract: FMplex introduces a model virtualization substrate for serving Foundation Models (FMs) by treating the FM backbone as a shared resource. It presents each downstream task with a virtual FM (vFM), allowing independent customization and lifecycle management while sharing the costly physical backbone. This approach significantly reduces memory waste and improves efficiency through optimized batching across colocated tasks.

papers.title: FuseFSS: Efficient Secure LLM Inference with Function Secret Sharing
papers.abstract: FuseFSS introduces a novel compiler for efficient two-server secure LLM inference using Function Secret Sharing (FSS). It replaces bespoke per-operator protocols with a unified compilation pipeline that compactly specifies fixed-point nonlinearities. This allows for batched FSS evaluations of packed comparisons and vector interval lookups, significantly improving efficiency over prior FSS-based methods.
papers.figure_caption: Gate-level activation microbench at L = 128 L{=}128 .

papers.title: Multi-Turn Evaluation of Deep Research Agents Under Process-Level Feedback
papers.abstract: This paper introduces a multi-turn evaluation framework to assess deep research agents' (DRAs) ability to improve based on feedback, moving beyond single-shot benchmarks. The core contribution is the **Research Gap Inference (RGI)** method, which analyzes rubric satisfaction to generate targeted, process-level feedback. This feedback significantly improves DRA performance, unlike self-reflection which shows negligible net gains.
papers.figure_caption: Process-level feedback generation. Given a report r t − 1 r_{t-1} evaluated against the DRACO rubric, RGI analyzes patterns of satisfied and unsatisfied criteria from FA, BD, and CQ (excluding PQ) to infer research-process gaps and generate process-level feedback f t − 1 f_{t-1} for the next turn. Example criteria shown for illustration; negative-weight criteria excluded for simplicity.

papers.title: Observability for Delegated Execution in Agentic AI Systems
papers.abstract: This paper addresses the challenge of tracking actions within specific delegation scopes in complex, agentic AI systems, where standard logs fail to distinguish between incompatible delegation assignments. The core method introduces an **agent-aware observability substrate** featuring a lightweight gateway and a common information model. This system binds execution traces to specific delegation contexts, enabling accurate **delegation-scoped attribution and access footprint reconstruction**.

papers.title: OmniGameArena: A Unified UE5 Benchmark for VLM Game Agents with Improvement Dynamics
papers.abstract: OmniGameArena introduces a unified benchmark using twelve diverse Unreal Engine 5 games (Solo, PvP, Coop) to evaluate Vision-Language Model (VLM) agents fairly. Its core contribution is the Improvement Dynamics Curve (IDC), a harness where a reflector LLM autonomously refines agent prompts across multiple rounds. This method provides not just a static score, but also the agent's score evolution and generalization ability across task variants.
papers.figure_caption: OmniGameArena at a glance. Twelve newly built UE5 games span Solo (7), PvP (3), and Coop (2) regimes (top). Heterogeneous agents (commercial VLMs, open-weight VLMs, keyboard-mouse policies, and gamepad policies) connect to the same real-time UE5 environment through documented adapters (middle). Evaluation reports the cold-start leaderboard and the Improvement Dynamics Curve (IDC) under multi-round reflection (bottom).

hacker_news.title: Apple reveals new AI architecture built around Google Gemini models
hacker_news.summary: Apple has unveiled a new artificial intelligence architecture that is built around and integrates Google's Gemini models.

hacker_news.title: Siri AI
hacker_news.summary: Apple has unveiled "Apple Intelligence," a suite of new on-device and cloud-based AI features deeply integrated across its operating systems, enhancing Siri and personal context understanding.

hacker_news.title: AI is slowing down
hacker_news.summary: The core content suggests that the rapid advancement and deployment of Artificial Intelligence are currently experiencing a slowdown.

hacker_news.title: Ask HN: What are tools you have made for yourself since the advent of AI?
hacker_news.summary: Hacker News users are discussing and sharing personal tools they have developed specifically in response to the rise of artificial intelligence.

hacker_news.title: Apple Core AI Framework
hacker_news.summary: Apple's Core ML framework allows developers to integrate trained machine learning models directly into their iOS, macOS, watchOS, and tvOS applications for on-device inference.

hacker_news.title: Microsoft's open source tools were hacked to steal passwords of AI developers
hacker_news.summary: Microsoft's open-source tools were compromised in a cyberattack designed to steal the login credentials of artificial intelligence developers.

hacker_news.title: FrontierCode
hacker_news.summary: Cognition AI introduced FrontierCode, a new AI model designed to significantly advance software engineering capabilities, aiming to revolutionize coding tasks.

hacker_news.title: Why are cells small?
hacker_news.summary: The core content of the story explores the fundamental biological reasons and physical constraints that determine and limit the size of individual cells.

hacker_news.title: Launch HN: Intuned (YC S22) – Build and run reliable browser automations as code
hacker_news.summary: Intuned (YC S22) offers a platform to build and execute reliable browser automations managed as code.

github_trending.description: AI agent skill that researches any topic across Reddit, X, YouTube, HN, Polymarket, and the web - then synthesizes a grounded summary
github_trending.summary: This AI agent skill researches current information across major platforms like Reddit, X, and YouTube to synthesize grounded summaries on any requested topic.

github_trending.description: A vector index built on TurboQuant, written in Rust with Python bindings
github_trending.summary: TurboVec is a Rust-based vector index leveraging TurboQuant, offering fast vector similarity search capabilities accessible via Python bindings.

github_trending.description: We write your reusable computer vision tools. 💜
github_trending.summary: Supervision provides reusable Python tools for computer vision workflows, offering functionalities like image preprocessing, annotation management, and model evaluation.
OpenCV is a comprehensive, open-source computer vision library providing a vast array of real-time image processing and computer vision algorithms for various applications.

github_trending.description: Desktop app to manage markdown knowledge bases
github_trending.summary: Tolaria is a desktop application designed to efficiently manage and organize markdown-based knowledge bases.

github_trending.description: an open source, extensible AI agent that goes beyond code suggestions - install, execute, edit, and test with any LLM
github_trending.summary: Goose is an open-source, extensible AI agent that goes beyond simple code suggestions, enabling users to install, execute, edit, and test code using any integrated Large Language Model.

github_trending.description: Find the local LLM that actually runs and performs best on your hardware. Ranked by real, recency-aware benchmarks, not parameter count. One command, run it instantly.
github_trending.summary: This repository provides a single-command tool to instantly benchmark and rank local Large Language Models based on real, recency-aware performance metrics relevant to your specific hardware, rather than just parameter count.
This repository serves as a centralized collection of PDF textbooks for all levels of Chinese education, spanning primary, secondary, and university courses.

github_trending.description: FULL Augment Code, Claude Code, Cluely, CodeBuddy, Comet, Cursor, Devin AI, Junie, Kiro, Leap.new, Lovable, Manus, NotionAI, Orchids.app, Perplexity, Poke, Qoder, Replit, Same.dev, Trae, Traycer AI, VSCode Agent, Warp.dev, Windsurf, Xcode, Z.ai Code, Dia & v0. (And other Open Sourced) System Prompts, Internal Tools & AI Models
github_trending.summary: This repository centralizes and shares system prompts, internal tools, and AI models for a wide array of AI coding assistants and development tools, including popular ones like Cursor, Devin AI, and NotionAI.
